description_long: This object is a dense cluster of oyster-like mushrooms with broad, overlapping caps that are a pale blue color. The caps are smooth and slightly convex, with the largest ones forming the uppermost layer and smaller ones arranged below. The gills are clearly visible from beneath, radiating outward in fine, closely packed lines and are a light grayish-white. The stems are short and mostly hidden, merging together at the base, giving the cluster a compact, bushy appearance. The overall form is organic and layered, with the caps fanning out horizontally and creating a tiered effect. The surface of the caps appears matte and soft, with no visible spots or scales.
description: A dense cluster of pale blue, smooth-capped mushrooms with visible gills and overlapping, tiered caps. The stems are short and mostly hidden, giving a bushy, organic appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cluster of overlapping blue caps and visible gills beneath the upper caps.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the horizontal layering of the caps and the compact, bushy structure.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the radiating gills and the convergence of short stems at the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the staggered arrangement of the caps and their smooth, convex surfaces.
